detailsText: Product details Keith Haring was one of the most renowned artists, filmmakers, and performers whose work responded to the urban street culture of the 1980s. Haring began exhibiting in galleries and museums around the world, while also continuing to participate in public projects, including literacy campaigns and anti-AIDS initiatives. Haring has been the subject of several international retrospectives. His work is in major private and public collections. © Keith Haring Foundation. Licensed by Artestar, New York. productDisplayName: Keith Haring Heavyweight Crop Zip Hoodie
detailsText: Product details Keith Haring was one of the most renowned artists, filmmakers, and performers whose work responded to the urban street culture of the 1980s. Haring began exhibiting in galleries and museums around the world, while also continuing to participate in public projects, including literacy campaigns and anti-AIDS initiatives. Haring has been the subject of several international retrospectives. His work is in major private and public collections. © Keith Haring Foundation. Licensed by Artestar, New York. Medium wash. Button-flap patch pockets at chest, slant welt pockets at hip. Button-tabs at rear hem. Responsibly Made: Made with 100% regenerative cotton, which is grown using holistic agricultural practices that improve soil health, climate resilience, water retention, and local biodiversity. Responsibly Made: This jacket is part of our water-saving Washwell program. Compared with conventional wash methods, Washwell uses at least 20% less water and has saved over a billion liters of water since 2016. This product was made in a factory that invests in gender equality and women's empowerment. Through RISE (Reimagining Industry to Support Equality) and Gap Inc.'s program P.A.C.E. detailsText: Product details Keith Haring was one of the most renowned artists, filmmakers, and performers whose work responded to the urban street culture of the 1980s. Haring began exhibiting in galleries and museums around the world, while also continuing to participate in public projects, including literacy campaigns and anti-AIDS initiatives. Haring has been the subject of several international retrospectives. His work is in major private and public collections. © Keith Haring Foundation. Licensed by Artestar, New York. Our exclusive, ultra-light Tencel™ blend with an easy drape for year-round wears. Fit: A full-length baggy jean that's fitted on the waist & relaxed all the way down. Fabric: 55% Cotton, 40% TENCEL, 5% Recycled Cotton. Stretch: No Stretch Jeans. Authentic denim that gets better with every wear. Made to wear all day & break in over time. Rise: Mid Rise Jeans. Look: A pull-on baggy jean in a light indigo wash. Details: Elasticized drawcord waist with faux fly & classic five-pocket styling. This pair of jeans is part of our water-saving Washwell program. Compared with conventional wash methods, Washwell uses at least 20% less water. Made with 55% Protocol cotton verified by the U.S. Cotton Trust Protocol, a program which works with farms committed to improving their environmental footprint by minimizing water usage and improving soil diversity (Claim No. 10127). Made with 5% recycled cotton. Compared to virgin materials, using recycled materials helps reduce resource use and waste. This product was made in a factory that invests in gender equity and women's empowerment through RISE. Learn more at gapinc.com/equity . Product #877815 Size & fit This Mid Rise Jean has a 10" (25cm) rise that's low slung in the hips. For a classic fit, go down one size. Fitted at the waist and hip. Loose, baggy through the leg. Full-length jean. Hits below the ankle. Inseam: Petite 28.5" (72 cm), Short 29.5" (75 cm), Regular 31" (79 cm), Long 33" (84 cm), Tall 35" (89 cm). Model is 5'9" and wearing size S. Fabric & care Cotton 55%, TENCEL™ Lyocell 40%, Recycled Cotton 5% Machine wash warm. Tumble dry low. Imported. Shipping & returns

detailsText: Product details Keith Haring was one of the most renowned artists, filmmakers, and performers whose work responded to the urban street culture of the 1980s.
